type: Step-Down to Medicine Floor Transfer Summary
author: Dr. Marcus Hong (PGY-3) / Dr. Jennifer Beckman (Medicine attending)
content:
TRANSFER FROM STEP-DOWN TO GENERAL MEDICINE FLOOR
Date/Time: 3/12/2027, 4:00 PM (Hospital Day 26)
Patient: Nguyen, Lan T (MRN-90215888)
From: Step-down unit 7E, bed 12
To: General Medicine floor 5N, bed 14
Interpreter: Vietnamese phone interpreter present at bedside for the transfer hand-off.

SUMMARY OF COURSE TO DATE (HD 26):

Ms. Nguyen is a 72-year-old Vietnamese-speaking woman who is now 26 days into an admission that began 2/15/2027 with severe acute biliary pancreatitis. Her course has been complicated as follows:

- HD 1 (2/15): Admitted for severe acute biliary pancreatitis; emergent ERCP next day with sphincterotomy and CBD stone extraction (E. coli grew from biliary fluid).
- HD 2-13 (2/16-2/27): MICU course for septic shock and moderate ARDS, intubated 2/17, extubated 2/27. Active TB ruled out (3 AFB smears + NAAT all negative). Course further complicated by AKI on CKD, which has resolved (Cr 1.3 today, at baseline).
- HD 13-22 (2/27-3/8): Step-down unit. Slow weaning from oxygen requirements; severe deconditioning from prolonged ventilation and immobility.
- HD 16 (3/2): NEW RLE DVT diagnosed (R common femoral through popliteal); started on heparin gtt then transitioned to apixaban 10 mg PO BID x 7 days now 5 mg PO BID maintenance (starting 3/9).
- HD 22-26 (3/8-3/12): Continued recovery on step-down. Off all supplemental O2 for 3 days; saturations 95-97% on room air. Tolerating regular diet (advanced from soft-mechanical 3/8). Mobilizing with PT — currently transfers with 1-person assist, ambulates ~25 feet with rolling walker and 1-person standby assist.

Reason for transfer to general medicine floor: stable hemodynamically, off O2, declared medically appropriate for floor-level care; awaiting PT/OT optimization and discharge planning for rehab placement.

VITALS AND EXAM TODAY:
Vitals (12 PM): BP 134/76, HR 82, RR 16, T 98.0 F, SpO2 96% RA. Weight 53.2 kg (down from admission 62 kg — significant ICU-acquired weakness and weight loss). BMI 22.4.
General: Pleasant, conversational through interpreter. Appearing weak and deconditioned but in no distress.
HEENT: No icterus. Mucous membranes moist.
CV: RRR. No murmur. Pulses 2+ in upper extremities. R DVT: residual mild RLE edema (grade 1+); no tenderness. L LE normal.
Lungs: CTAB. No crackles.
Abdomen: Soft, non-tender. Bowel sounds normal. Surgical scar from prior right TKA well-healed (extra-abdominal but noted for completeness).
MSK: Significant proximal muscle wasting in upper and lower extremities. Hip flexion 3/5, knee extension 3+/5 in both legs. Grip strength reduced.
Neuro: Alert, oriented; mildly slow with English-language commands but appropriate via interpreter. No focal deficits.

ACTIVE PROBLEMS LIST (on transfer):

#1 Severe acute biliary pancreatitis, resolving — labs normalized; cholecystectomy deferred (will be discussed with General Surgery again before discharge).
#2 Right lower extremity DVT (acute, proximal) — on apixaban 5 mg PO BID; goal 3 months minimum.
#3 Cholelithiasis — known. Awaiting elective cholecystectomy decision.
#4 ICU-acquired weakness / severe deconditioning — PT/OT involvement; planning rehab placement.
#5 Chronic kidney disease, stage 3a — recovered from AKI; Cr 1.3 at baseline; eGFR 47.
#6 Hypertension — restarted lisinopril 5 mg daily (3/10) at near-baseline Cr; BP well-controlled.
#7 Hyperlipidemia — restarted atorvastatin 40 mg daily on floor transfer.
#8 Latent tuberculosis infection — known. Active TB ruled out this admission. Continued declination of INH; documented in chart.
#9 Anemia — chronic disease; Hgb 9.2 today (transfused 1 unit PRBC 3/3 for Hgb 7.4 in setting of post-ICU). Tolerating apixaban without bleeding.
#10 Mild ICU delirium, resolving — now alert and oriented; no recurrence in 7 days off haloperidol.
#11 Nutrition — significant ICU-acquired weight loss (9 kg). Nutrition consult on the floor.

DISCHARGE PLANNING (for the floor team):
- Anticipate discharge to acute rehabilitation facility in approximately 5-7 days if stable.
- Medicare/Medicaid dual-eligible; insurance approval for IRF or SAR pending — case management engaged 3/10.
- Family: son Quang (primary caregiver) and daughter-in-law Linh will accompany at discharge. Vietnamese-language discharge instructions critical.
- Pre-existing PCP: Dr. Phong Le at Charm City Asian Health Center.
- Anticipated outpatient follow-ups:
  • Dr. Le (PCP) within 1 week of discharge.
  • General Surgery (Dr. Markovich) to discuss elective cholecystectomy.
  • Hematology (Dr. Pishko) at 1 month for DVT/anticoagulation management.
  • Nephrology consideration if CKD progresses further.

MEDICATION RECONCILIATION (current):
- Apixaban 5 mg PO BID (since 3/9; full 3-month course minimum)
- Lisinopril 5 mg PO daily (resumed 3/10)
- Atorvastatin 40 mg PO daily (resumed today)
- Acetaminophen 500-1000 mg PO q6h PRN pain
- Calcium 600 mg PO daily + Vit D3 1000 IU daily
- Multivitamin daily
- Pantoprazole 40 mg PO daily (started in ICU for stress ulcer ppx; will reassess for ongoing need)
- Potassium chloride 20 mEq PO daily (started 3/2 for ICU-related hypokalemia)
- HELD: ibuprofen (CKD + anticoagulation); avoid going forward.

ALLERGIES: PCN (childhood rash, tolerated pip/tazo this admission); iodinated contrast (urticarial reaction 2019 — pre-medication required for any future contrast).

DOCUMENTATION FOR FOLLOWING TEAM:
The step-down team has reviewed all systems. Family is well-informed and on board with current management. Code status FULL CODE. Healthcare proxy: verbal designation son Quang; formal designation pending discharge.

Thank you to MICU + Step-down + ID + Hematology + Pulm + GI + General Surgery teams for collaborative care. Floor team please contact step-down with any questions during transition.
attestation:
Electronically signed by:
Dr. Marcus Hong, MD (PGY-3, Internal Medicine) — transferring team
Dr. Jennifer Beckman, MD (Attending, Internal Medicine)
Date: 3/12/2027
